propositions: Operation certificates are currently local self-attestations signed with a per-clone symmetric HMAC secret, verifiable only with the forge-capable secret, so they are not enforceable third-party controls.
Migrate operation certificates to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures attributed to the signing environment so anyone can verify against that environment's public key without a shared secret.

A project can optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment, pinning its public key out-of-band (review-gated, not the tickets branch) and verifying against the pinned key rather than the self-claimed key_id.
A merge-gate CI job verifies the required-environment certificate over the merged log and fails when a ticket that must carry one is missing or foreign.
A trusted rebar-mcp remote server must fetch authoritative ticket state and code-at-commit itself and run the gate on that, so client-supplied doctored inputs cannot forge a passing verdict.

Certificates bind ticket id, material fingerprint, and merged-log position to prevent replay, and environment-key rotation uses era-scoped validity; the legacy HMAC scheme is removed via expand-contract migration.
